items.text: lume review ingest writes each review_gap as an OPEN gap record via gap.add_gap at ingest time (source = repo name, context = review slug + why_missed + proposed_change, created from the clock seam); proposed_workstreams and direction_decisions remain print-only

check.cmd: grep -rq 'captured_gaps' plugin/src/lume/ && ! grep -q 'lume gap add' plugin/src/lume/review.py

items.text: The printed queue plan no longer contains 'lume gap add' lines (they are executed, not proposed); the human output reports the captured gap ids, and --json carries a captured_gaps list (id per review_gap)

check.cmd: python3 -m pytest -q tests/test_review_ingest.py 2>/dev/null | tail -1 | grep -q passed

items.text: Captured gaps are NOT auto-linked to any workstream (triage stays the operator's); a re-ingest captures its own new records (no rerun dedupe in v0.1, per the F3 decision)

Tests cover: gaps created on ingest with the review-slug context, empty review_gaps creates none, queue plan contains no gap add lines, --json captured_gaps shape; full suite green with no assertion weakened
Docs updated: SKILL.md and plugin/README describe auto-captured review_gaps (and that workstreams/decisions stay print-only); the F3 supersession is reflected, not contradicted
Loop residue (0019 close flips, 0020 planning acceptance) committed; .lume/gaps and .lume/reviews clean at handback

dod.preamble: P1 of the 0020 plan (G6): review_gaps become real open gap records at ingest time via the existing gap mechanic; the queue plan slims to the still-gated items (workstreams, decisions).

handback: P1 (G6) shipped: review_gaps become open gap records at ingest time, tagged with the review slug, never auto-linked; the printed queue plan now carries only the operator-gated items (workstreams, decisions); --json carries captured_gaps. Protocol mapping text + both docs updated; suite 418 green; all DoD checks pass. One assertion inverted by design (gaps ARE now created on ingest) - detailed in the self-review.
self_review: All six DoD checks pass (0 failed / 6); suite 418 passed (the never-creates-gaps test was reshaped into never-creates-workstreams-but-captures-gaps, plus a new no-dedupe re-ingest test - one assertion deliberately INVERTED to match the F3 supersession, not weakened: workstream non-creation is still asserted). Implementation is small and reuses the gap mechanic wholesale: add_gap at ingest with a gap_context helper; queue_commands loses its review_gaps branch and documents why; the emitted protocol's mapping text was also updated (caught by a DoD check - it would otherwise have contradicted the new behaviour). Honest notes: (1) capture happens after findings/store writes - if add_gap somehow failed mid-list, earlier gaps would persist with the review captured but the command exiting nonzero; acceptable for v0.1 (validated input, local writes) and consistent with no-rerun-dedupe. (2) The review_result schema and emit-side golden tests needed no change - capture is ingest-side only.
